articles.summary: Your mail piece has about three seconds to earn a second look — before it goes from the mailbox to the recycling bin.

articles.title: AI CONTENT IS EVERYWHERE ON SOCIAL MEDIA, ESPECIALLY LINKEDIN
articles.summary: AI-generated content is spreading across social media, with longform posts hit the hardest. Pangram data from over 1M posts shows 13.8% flagged as AI, rising to 25% for posts over 250 words. LinkedIn drives nearly two-thirds of flagged content, with over 40% of longform posts fully AI-generated. X shows the highest combined AI use when including mixed content. Reddit replies remain mostly human, but top-level posts show higher AI rates, revealing gaps in moderation.

articles.title: OPENAI APPEARS TO BE MISSING ITS SALES GOALS BY A VAST MARGIN
articles.summary: OpenAI's $100 billion ad-revenue target for 2030 requires three things to happen at once. Advertisers moving decades of search and social budgets into chatbots, OpenAI out-competing Google and Meta for that spend, and the entire AI ad market growing roughly 100 times from where it sits today. The category's entire addressable market currently tops out around $5.4B, and OpenAI itself is on pace to miss its own ad revenue forecast by about 90% this year.

articles.title: COPYWRITING BREAKDOWN: HOW TO SELL SOMETHING ORDINARY
articles.summary: Strong marketing often comes from better research instead of better copy. Claude Hopkins won customers in a crowded category by asking hundreds of consumers what they wanted, measuring how Palmolive performed against those needs, and turning the results into specific proof points. The ad replaced vague claims with numbers such as 250 times more lather, 15% water absorption in one minute, and lather that lasted 10 minutes. Research, measurable evidence, and clear messaging can make an ordinary product stand out even when competitors offer similar features.

articles.title: DON'T BLOCK PERSONAL EMAILS
articles.summary: Blocking personal emails in B2B forms can hurt conversion rates. Instead, detect domains like Gmail or iCloud and require a company URL field. With name and company, you can still find or enrich work emails. This keeps friction low while preserving lead quality. Many high-intent leads still submit personal emails, often due to autofill behavior.

articles.title: SWAG THAT WORKS: THE NEW RULES FOR TRADE SHOW GIVEAWAYS
articles.summary: 47% of marketers already treat branded merchandise as a core marketing channel, and 87% of recipients keep and use what they receive rather than tossing it. Middesk built a tiered swag strategy around that shift at Money20/20, gating higher-value items behind booth actions like booking a sales meeting or agreeing to a case study, then generated 10 times its activation spend in new pipeline and 26 times more in influenced pipeline. The gifting also produced a surprise: a steep jump in LinkedIn followers driven entirely by pins and luggage tags that were never meant to be the campaign's main goal.

articles.title: REACH 7 MILLION ENGAGED TECH PROFESSIONALS
articles.summary: Ads get ignored on social media but not in TLDR! Reach developers, PMs, marketers, founders and other tech leaders where they actually

articles.title: pay attention. Learn more about sponsorship opportunities.
OPAL U: BECOME A CERTIFIED AI EXPERT IN 5 DAYS
articles.summary: Opal U is a free 5-day live cohort for senior marketers who need to prove AI ROI and move fast. Built for VPs and heads of marketing who feel stuck in the hype and need practical output. Spend one hour a day building three AI agents that automate real work and save 10+ hours a week.

articles.title: HOW THE FOUNDER OF MORNING BREW BUILT A CLAUDE CONTENT MACHINE THAT NEVER RUNS OUT OF IDEAS (43 MINUTE VIDEO)
articles.summary: Alex Lieberman's Content Machine scans seven days of Slack, Notion, Gmail, and meeting notes, plus the accounts he follows on X and LinkedIn, then ranks 15 content ideas a day so the blank page never becomes the bottleneck. Six AI personas modeled on interviewers like Tim Ferriss and Howard Stern question him until they extract real specifics, and his voice-to-texted answers trace every sentence back to something he actually said. Before anything posts, a six-persona Writer's Council scores the draft and blocks it from going below a 9 out of 10.

articles.title: HOW MUCH CREATIVE DEBT ARE YOU CARRYING?
articles.summary: Creative work needs recurring time on the calendar or it will always lose to urgent tasks. "Creative debt" is the cost of repeatedly delaying creative projects until avoiding them becomes part of your identity instead of a temporary choice. Treat creative projects like recurring commitments by scheduling dedicated time, reduce project scope, and keep momentum through small, consistent actions instead of waiting for the perfect moment. You should also remove pressure to prove ROI so creative work can exist without immediate business value. The goal is to stop delaying meaningful projects before postponement blocks future creative output.

articles.title: WHY YOUR TOP KEYWORDS ARE QUIETLY LOSING VOLUME
HOW I (ACCIDENTALLY?) WENT FROM LIFECYCLE MARKETER TO PRODUCT MANAGER
articles.summary: A lifecycle marketer transitioned into a product manager role through embedded product work, not a planned switch. Leading iterative in-app projects and using AI to prototype solutions shifted ownership from execution to problem definition. Internal support helped formalize the move. The role change requires giving up solo execution and focusing on defining problems and aligning teams. Lifecycle skills translate well, especially prioritization and building user experiences.

articles.title: AI SEO TIP: MICROSOFT CLARITY TOPIC INSIGHTS
articles.summary: Microsoft Clarity Topic Insights is a free AI visibility tool that shows how often your content is cited in AI-generated answers, how much it influences those answers, and which competitors are being cited.

articles.title: 10 CREATIVE WAYS TO WRITE WITH AI WITHOUT LOSING YOUR SOUL
articles.summary: Tips on how Ahrefs uses AI to create content, plus the experiments they've been running.

articles.title: HOW MICHELOB ULTRA AND LEO MESSI PREPARED FOR THE MOST UNPREDICTABLE MARKETING MOMENT OF THE WORLD CUP
articles.summary: Michelob Ultra built its Messi tribute ad around 20 years of career accomplishments, letting the same creative work whether Argentina won or lost the final.
